titleStmt.title: SII 23.583: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 583. (A.R. No. 583 of 1907.) Nandalūru, Rajampet Taluk, Cuddapah District Saumyanātha temple—on the 2nd tier of the same base [Vikrama-Chōḷa]. Year 3: 1120-21 A.D.

summary.p: The inscription is built in at the beginning and is also incomplete. The introduction is that beginning with the words Pūmādu puṇara. It is dated in Śaka 104[2], Śārvari, Chittirai-Vishu, when Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Bettarasa was ruling Pottappi-nāḍu. The purport of the record is not clear except that the chief founded an agrahāra for the success of the king's arms. Some reference is made to a parigraha (seizure ?) in which two or three minor chieftains seem to have been involved.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
—ppat taṉ tirupatumalar maṉnavar cūṭa maṉniya Urimaiyil maṇimuṭi cūṭic ceṅkol ticaitoṟuñ cella veṅkalikaṭin=tu meyyaṟan taḻaippakkaliṅka miriya kaṭarmalainaṭātti valam kolāḻi varai Ūḻi naṭā
—ḷoṭum viṟṟirun=taruḷiya kopparakesari panmarāṉa cakka coḻadevaṟkku yāṇṭu muṉṟāvatu Ipperumāḷ śrībhujaṅkaḷ varddhikka Agrahāram paṇṇinen . svasticaraṇasaroruha vihita vilocana
—kuśanayyanasimha śrīmanmahāmaṇḍaleśvara pettarasaṉ pottappi nāṭāḷāniṟkka śakavarṣam Āyirattu nāṟppattu Ira[ ṇṭāvatu śā] rvari samvatsarattu citraiviṣuvi nāḷ nāṉ Adhirājendramaṇṭalattu meṟ
—nāṭṭil Imayatte Iraṭṭakuṭiyum kāliṅkanāṭāḻvāṉum ṭināṭāḻvāṉum mattappatte Iraṭṭakuṭiyu muḷḷiṭṭa Innāṭṭu Iraṭṭakuṭikkuṭaiyavum parigrahattil IruraṇappeṟkuṭiyumpennamaStops here.
Digital edition of SII 23.583 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
